rulings.Case Title: YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL VS. CRAIG K MARTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 2: CROSS DEFENDANT JUDY EASHMAN-BOWERS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. As to the third cause of action, Cross-Complainant alleges facts with the requisite specificity to state a claim for intentional misrepresentation. (See Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) As to the demurrer on the ground that the entire Second Amended Verified Cross-Complaint ("SACC") is vague because the exhibit was not attached to the copy served on moving party, overruled as moot. Cross-Complainant has served another copy of the SACC on counsel for moving party with the exhibit attached. In the future, counsel/self represented party should contact each other directly to address and resolve, if possible, apparent errors. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT WILLIAMS VS. U.S. BANCORP INVESTMENTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP INVESTMENTS, INC. & U.S. BANCORP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for unpaid business expenses. Plaintiff does not contest dismissal of this claim. (Pl's Opp. to Demurrer to First Amended Complaint; p. 1, line 18-21.) The demurrer is overruled as to all other causes of action. Collateral estoppel does not apply here, as the first prong of the test requiring that the issues be identical is not established. (Johnson v. GlaxoSmithKline, Inc. (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1497, 1507-08.) The Burakoff class was comprised of "all individuals who are or were employed as Investment Financial Consultants in the State of California at any time during the period commencing October 13, 2001 to [May 8, 2008]." (May 25, 2011 Order re: Defendant US Bancorp's Motion for Decertification in LASC Case no. BC341430.) The putative class in this case is comprised of "all commission paid employees who worked for Defendants in California from May 9, 2008 until the date of certification." (First Amended Complaint, 15.) The issue of whether the class in this case can be certified is not identical to the issue of decertification of the Burakoff class because the two classes are comprised of different class members during different time periods. Alvarez v. May Dept. Stores Co. ((2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1223, 1237) and Johnson v. GlaxoSmithKline, Inc. ((2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1497, 1507-08), both cited by Defendants in support of their collateral estoppel argument, state that collateral estoppel applies where a party seeks to certify an identical class. Because the class here is not identical, collateral estoppel does not apply. Additionally, Plaintiff has sufficiently pled that his claims are typical of all class members. (First Amended Complaint, 17(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT WILLIAMS VS. U.S. BANCORP INVESTMENTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration To Pltfs' Individual Claims And To Dismiss The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANTS U.S. BANCORP INVESTMENTS, INC. & U.S. BANCORP'S Motion To Compel Arbitration To PlAINtIFfs' Individual Claims And To Dismiss The First Amended Complaint. Denied without prejudice. Defendants' Requests for Judicial Notice are granted. Plaintiff's objections are overruled. FINRA Rule 13204(d) prohibits the enforcement of an arbitration agreement with respect to any claim that is the subject of a putative class action until there is a determination of class certification made. Collateral estoppel does not apply here, as the first prong of the test requiring that the issues be identical is not established. (Johnson v. GlaxoSmithKline, Inc. (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1497, 1507-08.) The Burakoff class was comprised of "all individuals who are or were employed as Investment Financial Consultants in the State of California at any time during the period commencing October 13, 2001 to [May 8, 2008]." (May 25, 2011 Order re: Defendant US Bancorp's Motion for Decertification in LASC Case no. BC341430.) The putative class in this case is comprised of "all commission paid employees who worked for Defendants in California from May 9, 2008 until the date of certification." (First Amended Complaint, 15.) The issue of whether the class in this case can be certified is not identical to the issue of decertification of the Burakoff class because the two classes are comprised of different class members during different time periods. Alvarez v. May Dept. Stores Co. ((2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1223, 1237) and Johnson v. GlaxoSmithKline, Inc. ((2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 1497, 1507-08), both cited by Defendants in support of their collateral estoppel argument, state that collateral estoppel applies where a party seeks to certify an identical class. Because the class here is not identical, collateral estoppel does not apply. Therefore, no determination of class certification has been made at this point in the proceedings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: STEVE RYAN VS. MITCHELL ROSENFELD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANTs MITCHELL ROSENFELD, SACHIKO ROSENFELD & MOEJOE PROPERTIES LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendants' motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all causes of action. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. Resolution of the statute of limitations issue as a matter of law is proper only if the uncontradicted facts are susceptible to one legitimate inference. (Jolly v. Eli Lilly & Co. (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103, 1112.) Even though Defendant shifted the burden as to the first cause of action, Plaintiff produces evidence that he did not discover the alleged wrongful conduct of the Defendants until June 2009. (See Evidence Cited in Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts in Opposition to Motion for Summary Adjudication of Issues ("PSSUF"), Exh. 2, ("Ryan Decl.") pg. 6:17-28, Exh. B.) As such, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the statute of limitations has expired. As to the second cause of action, summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to when Plaintiff discovered Defendant Mitchell Rosenfeld's breach of fiduciary duty as a partner, which is subject to a 4 year statute of limitation under CCP §343. (See Ryan Decl. pg. 6:17-28, Exh. B.) Even if Plaintiff's claim for breach of fiduciary duty as his attorney was time-barred under CCP §340.6, summary adjudication would still be denied because it does not completely dispose the cause of action. (See CCP §437c(f)(1).) Summary adjudication is denied as to the fourth and fifth causes of action as there are triable issues of material fact as to whether the statute of limitations expired. (See Ryan Decl., pg. 12:1-19, Exh. L, 23-26; pg. 12:21-28, Exh. M, 27-28.) =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: CLARCEEN CALDWELL VS. WALTER SCOTT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Req For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 10: DEFENDANT WALTER SCOTT's motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents. Denied. As to Request for Production nos. 18 - 20, pursuant to which defendants seek retainer agreements, these agreements are privileged pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code §§ 6068(e) and 6169 and Evid. Code § 952. Plaintiffs have not waived any privilege. As to Request for Production nos. 23 - 25, Plaintiffs have not put their financial information at issue. Further, it is protected by the constitutional right to privacy (Cal. Const. Art. 1, § 1), and, in any event, moving parties have not shown how even under a balancing test such documents should be compelled here. Monetary sanctions were not requested, and none are rewarded. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: FRESCO MUSIC, LLC VS. ALJOY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton For A Protective Order Precluding Pltf From Introducing Expert Testimony At Trial, Or Alternatively, Compelling Disclosure Of Expert Witness Information
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 3: CROSS COMPLAINANTS ALJOY, INC.'S AND PETER GLIKSHTERN'S Motion For A Protective Order Precluding Plaintiff From Introducing Expert Testimony At Trial, Or Alternatively, Compelling Disclosure Of Expert Witness Information Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY PARTY INTENDS TO RELY ON EXPERT TESTIMONY AT TRIAL, THE PARTY WILL .DISCLOSE THE NAME(S) OF THE EXPERT(S) AND A SUMMARY OF THE ANTICIPATED TESTIMONY NO LATER THAN 5:00 PM ON JANUARY 8, 2014. ANY EXPERT DISCLOSED BY PLAINTIFF WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE FOR DEPOSITION NO LATER THAN 5:00 PM ON JANUARY 15, 2014. ANY EXPERT DISCLOSED BY DEFENDANTS WILL BE MADE AVAILABLE FOR DEPOSITION NO LATER THAN 5:00 PM ON JANUARY 17, 2014. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WERE SOUGHT AND NONE WILL BE IMPOSED. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 6, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MARIA HENRIQUEZ VS. RODRICK FOODS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 18: DEFENDANT RODRICK FOODS, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion to strike the request for punitive damages on paragraph 14, page 3 of the Complaint is granted. No opposition filed. The motion was previously continued from September 23, 2013 to October 23, 2013 for lack of courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by Local Rule 6.2B. The matter was then taken off calendar on October 23, 2013 for non-compliance with the Court's order to provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL PADWAY, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MICHAEL VS. NAPOLI, BERN,RIPKA & ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 5: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL PADWAY, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MICHAEL PADWAY & ASSOCIATES' Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO PRODUCE THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR ATTORNEYS IN THE AMOUNT OF $250.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ROCKY POINT PARTNERS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. ERIN THOMPSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 6, 2014, Line 22: DEFENDANT ERIN THOMPSON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the first cause of action on the grounds of uncertainty is overruled. Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to plead facts, it if it can do so in good faith, that establish the existence of a fiduciary relationship. Defendant's request to strike punitive damages cannot be the subject of a demurrer, and, in any event, is not requested in the notice of hearing.
